idea-plugin.name: Tests Analyzer
idea-plugin.description:
Runs tests and visualizes their execution timeline to identify parallelism issues and slow tests.

Main features

  • Gantt Chart — timeline showing each test as a horizontal bar, making it obvious which tests run in parallel and which are sequential bottlenecks.
  • Results Table — sortable, filterable table with class name, test name, duration and color-coded status.
  • HTML Export — self-contained HTML report with interactive chart, filtering, and light/dark theme.

How to use

  • Right-click a test directory or file in the Project View.
  • Select Tests Analyzer → Run and Analyze to execute tests and view results.
  • Select Tests Analyzer → Analyze from Existing Execution to load previously saved results.

Supported build systems

  • Gradle — automatic module and test task detection via RunConfiguration API and SMTestRunner.
  • Maven — runs tests and parses Surefire/Failsafe XML reports.

Supported test frameworks

  • JUnit 5, Kotest, Spock 2.x, TestNG

GitHub | Issue Tracker
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.0
  • Initial release: run tests and show results with table and timeline chart.

action.description: Run tests and display results with timing analysis
Read test results from the build folder without running tests
